Weight & Packaging
Water test ✅
Depositor #1 making adjustments
Mid shift

It is good that we will be reviewing safety chain checks during spec reviews moving forward.
 
QA checks can certainly be added, but where can we find the specific checks required for each product?
 
It would be beneficial for everyone to be aligned on both the required checks and the reasoning behind them. In this particular case, adding additional checks may actually introduce more risk rather than reduce it.
Line down for scraper fault

Good morning Kimberly Morales

Is that the back off plow section or a scraper somewhere else on the line?
Update 3rd Shift
Sorry for the late update they figured out the issue and have been running
Sensor cleaned, line is back up

Thanks Frederick Peairs. 

Kimberly Morales actually posted at 4:48am that the line was back up. 

I was clarifying the location of the fault and details around it.
Tg3 shift 3 finish

Continuously run.
TG3 had issues this morning with a broken door not closing and stopping the machine. Maintenance fixed that.

Tempering chart check update 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.
Countdown.
Kimberly Morales can you please talk to your teams about the water cups?
Yes, which desk?
Julissa took care of it!
The packaging passed the water test:
Problem with the air in FW
Air compressor was faulted out. Maintenance reset it and the line is running.
The weight, texture, appearance, surface, and packaging remain within the technical specifications:
No open or empty pieces observed.

Ya mantenimiento lo soluciono

First two pictures are the original set points that I had made on Friday and the second two pictures are the ones I made earlier today. The line has been running great at the first aligner. Only problems that I noticed were from the Demolder. I recommend cleaning before going and adjusting conveyor speeds please.
No open or empty pieces are observed.

1 Shift
Verified Lot:
Line is running
Line is down. Working with maintenance making a New recipe because QA has different weight for caramel and almond
we finished the production PRS DARK

Change over is ready
Ítem 30735
We started running at 11:20, item number 30735 PRS truffles
There are 2 tables missing on the line, Molding 4 lent us one since it was needed to support the boxes when putting on the labels
2nd shift update. Production meets all quality requirements. Pouch seal integrity is in good conditions. 10 wrappers are inside the pouch. Lot and BB date has been verified. No issues to report.
Starting up checklist
Scissor and pick are missing
The line started at time
Production meets all quality requirements. Pouch seal integrity is in good conditions. 10 wrappers are inside the pouch. Lot and BB date has been verified. No issues to report.
It was checked during maintenance; the plate was very tight, but it has now been loosened and is picking up heat from the molds.
At 11:43 AM, the deposit process begins.
The temperature of the inclusion mixer water has been maintained at 93, allowing the line to run correctly.
The Imperial Belt is moving,  Maintenance is checking it.
Metal detector and c6 belt are running well
The product's weight is within the range.
The Organoleptic testing: appearance, taste, texture and odor are acceptable
Startup and shutdown checklist have this step in them to ensure the water drain valve is closed.
The flow wrap meets specifications and lote code are correct
We already have the blue wrap on the line
Metal detector and c6 belt are running well
Glycol is good
Second shift verification
Centerline

We ended up with three accumulated bins because the conveyor belt C6 became misaligned. While the maintenance team worked to realign the belt, additional chocolate was being spilled; ultimately, we were unable to pass all of the chocolate, as it was heavily scratched and failed to meet our quality standards. that's why at the end we have a lot at scrap.
Capture test results
Start up checklist

The hose doesn’t look completely secure, be careful when moving it Grey Boscan, Raoul Lubain if we go down we can try secure it better. Also the ground cable for the nut hopper wasn’t attached we hooked that up though
The temper increased to 0.7; we have stopped filling with chocolate and are taking a new temper reading.
Have we decreased mixing water yet?
That's right—we reduced it by 0.2 points.
We started to put chocolate in the molds because we got 0.6
We got a .7 stopped for high temper retested now
.5 temper starting back up
I need this magnet strainer back for Sollich line 3, so I am ordering a replacement that doesn't have the water jacket on it. It will arrive on Wednesday and then we can swap it out.
Pallet verification
Second pallet verification
Metal detector and c6 belt are running well

I had an action to calculate when we will run out of FW. On a good day between all 3 shifts we will produce an average of 360k (this varies a lot though). with scrap around 18k daily consumption 378k at that rate it’ll last us 4 days

The position of the pipe looks to have changed normally it’s further north. 

Which is what I believe is causing it to drip out of the sweco
We repositioned the pipe and will report if there’s anymore issues
Weight of the bars are within target range spec. There are 2 or more almonds in a bar! Lot is present and legible on the flow wrap! Appearance of the bars is acceptable
Metal detector and c6 belt are running well
Verification of the production line process: piece weight, organoleptic properties, Flow Wrap packaging, and tempering—ensuring all fall within the correct range.
Test capture correct
Demolding, metal detector, and C6 belts are running correctly
Final verification of the super sack and correct final label
Camera alarm on QA, maintenance, and supervisors are here to inspect
No issues found it was dirty pin
End of super sack Lot 61AUC1-2; Lot change to 61BUC1-3; start of super sack confirmed.
Piston failure maintenance has been called
Maintenance is on it
Piston fault has been fixed
Piston faulted again
Maintenance is working on it
End of Super Sack Lot 61BUC1-3; Start of New Super Sack Lot 61BUC1-3
Piston is still down on the imperial maintenance is still working on it
Still down empting hopper and mixer. Still down maintenance is still working on it
Midshift
Washing nozzles
Pulling dosing head to clean
washing dosing head now
Raoul Lubain make sure the SU checklist is followed when starting back up and please don't deposit any chocolate until everything is in temper
ATP plate and rolled correct
Thank you for reminding me. I already talked to the team about it.
putting dosing block and nozzle block back together then in
Following su check list checking temps
Temp check good
back up and running
Imperial down piston again maintenance is on it
Final verification of supersack and label for Lot 61BUC1-3, Date 05/02/2027: Correct.
Piston faulted again maintenance is working on
I have ordered a bead roller so we can put a bead on the end of this tube that will keep it inplace when clamped.
Demolding, metal detector, and C6 belt are running correctly
The night shift had problems with the hopper plate, the right pistons were not moving.
For now, we can see the plate contains a lot of almond, which is causing it to be clogged, Maintenance is currently checking it.
Countdown

The label is experiencing issues, as it is tearing easily while the product is running.

Production will continue using it at this time. The process will remain under observation, and monitoring will continue to determine if the issue persists.
Charity Young, Mary Montilla, Miriah Manzanares
MMk film rolls that break easily are being used on the label machine and are working well.
1 shift
The labeling machine has started malfunctioning; the roller that pulls the labels is not rotating. Maintenance is here, and we have started stacking the jars on pallets.
S2 Enrober Elizabeth Hernandez do we know why those setting were not recorded on the CL?
Let me speak with the operator, and I'll let you know.

Already talked to him, and looks like we have a couple of duplicated settings, I’m going to do some corrections to the CL. 
Justin Garner FYI as well

There are still pallets of item 20001 and 20001-REWORK in the WH that have not been considered for use in production. Instead, the caramel coming off line S2 has been used in the kitchen. We need to consume the caramel in buckets following FIFO. Curt Willis, Charity Young, Dakota Adair, Katya Farnsworth, Jody Howse, Miriah Manzanares. Please your help with this.
Curt Willis Miriah Manzanares Charity Young please your help with this.

Hi. 
I’ll follow up on this in the morning, but Dakota since Curt is out today, can you tell me why production is not using it? 
Is it on Hold?
Angelica can you tell me if this material is on hold for failed testing? ^^

We have been using the caramel that comes off the line to minimize the accumulation of caramel buckets. if we use prior caramel that was made we will just continue to create caramel buckets. Miriah Manzanares. If needed we can use the caramel in the racking's if it is approved to be used. Nidia Lopez Corea
The set screws were loose and allowing it to slip
Maintenance adjusted the blue roller, and it is now working correctly.
I just checked our hold log. I don’t see any 20001-rework on hold.
Jody Howse Brandon Hernandez Joseph Scott, Luis Falcon FYI.
Shift 3

Good morning! 


Can we please re-evaluate the spec and make sure that everything is right. We can not have a min higher than the max on chocolate. Please advise Candon Watt Jessica Malave Anisa Rynearson Curt Willis

Brought the max reject to 650, we’re seeing bags hit 85-90. 

However, the average piece count at 6.5, should have us lower(closer to target) while still hitting our piece count. 

We’re zeroing the MM3 now to see if that helps.
We have counted different bags and we maintain a range of 83-86 pieces per bag
85 is our minimum piece weight Delia Zambrano Conner Boyd
Ok

We’ve raised the Yamato to 630, and put the check weigher to 670. 

Our most recent bag had 83 pieces at 620g on Yamato.
Shortly after we set the Yamoto to(630g) we had a 77 bag count, so we’ve adjusted the Yamoto to (650g) to see if that gets us 85 pieces at stable rate.
Once we have the chocolate per piece down, we’ll return to lowering the Yamoto and Check weigher numbers.

We paused the line for 3 minutes in order to clean the bottom of the blower and confirm there wasn't any buildup of chocolate. We also reduced the screw speed for the enrober from 22 to 20. We'll post the final piece weights once these changes reach the back end. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
The metal detector's belt is broken
Enrober EoS

Sollich 3a
The Cute Cavanna isn't working; it's showing this error. We've already called maintenance and are currently waiting.
Chris. he part has been repaired and is being sanitized.
Good morning Grey Boscan, yes that is correct.  Those are the qty's needed.   Thank You
Blade Pass ATP
The line has started.
Temper in the range
Pre-piece in the range
Final piece in the range.  Appearance is good
One of the conveyors isn't working properly; we've already notified maintenance.
Maintenance continues working on the Cavanna cutter and the conveyor.
The Cute Cavana is now operational, but the jaws are moving excessively; this is causing the film alignment to become misconfigured. The maintenance team continues to work on the conveyor.

Good afternoon Rich Brierley

I love that we are filling out CL sheets. 

Need to see them circle when things are out. Also when we run well for a couple shifts let’s get the top section filled out as the standard.

This conveyor is out of service. The original motor got very hot from being overworked so swapped out the motor from a conveyor that is not being used currently to see if that would help but it did not make any difference. After troubleshooting it further it needs a more powerful motor considering the belt size and weight being loaded onto the conveyor. The conveyor runs fine until weight is added even at 100% power it can't handle it.
The cute little Cavana is already running well.
How fast is it running? It should be rated to 50 lbs, which is 1600 ppm. As long as it is running at at least 7 fpm it shouldn't get overloaded.
I'll try to get exact numbers tomorrow but had no luck with it. The other issue is the angle it's trying to lift that weight so it won't budge once fully loaded.
Cutie Cavanna needed a power cycle and film timing adjustments on 1st shift to get it to work.
Nathan Gevenois
Changing over to UTT dark.
Lot has been verified. No previous product was found
Line is running
Sorry about that, eyemark is centered I just took the first samples to verify the lots but I will send a picture soon of how it’s currently looking.
Thank you! Let’s make sure the black eyemark is centered please.

Pallet verification 
Item: 20128 PRS Dark Bite
Here are how the eye marks are looking

Could we adjust the timer for how long the metal detector opens during a metal check? Currently, it's staying open too long and dropping an excessive number of truffles. Reducing the open time could help minimize rework and scrap, as the truffles become scuffed when they fall into the reject bin. At the moment, the entire bin is filling up during each metal check.

I don’t remember in L1 having too much product rejecting. 

Spencer Andrus Preston Elmer Marcos Montano
Pallet verification UTT dark truf bite
Pre piece and final piece of the truffle are within target range. The appearance is smooth and acceptable. Lot and BB are present and legible on the flow wrappers.

I followed up with the lead she said there was problems on the cute cavanna with open FW but got better after the adjustment made

Machine shut down and restarted
All checks on foreign materials were successfully completed, and the product passed the inspection based on established quality and safety standards.

Hi Team,
Upon arrival, I noticed that the vanilla pump configuration was not correct due to the power outage on Friday. I reset it, and it is now working properly.👇
How will that impact the caramel made during and after the power outage on Friday?

Hi Chris J. or Chris G.,
I would like to ask one of you to please come to the Conbar at Sollich 4 Kitchen.
Thank you
Chris Givens Chris Jeffs

Good question. I don’t think it affected the water activity, only the viscosity, which was lower than expected. I suggest taking viscosity samples again and checking the flavor. In my opinion, that would be the best approach.
S4 Conbar before
After

Hi Team. I'm trying to verify my numbers for BEQ, but I see 95,060 pieces currently in Hold status. Will these be released, or should I exclude them from my count? Miriah Manzanares, Hervin Herrera, Alan Todd
Jorel Gourdin correct me if I am wrong. The customer will let us use those mixing them with the good ones.
Correct. There were 2 pallets from the first day of production. I'm not sure if anything else was put on hold besides those.
I verify and totally forgot to update, there were 3 pallets. A deviation will be written so they know they need to mix them

Hervin Herrera,

Maria Moron Quintero looked and there are 6 pallets on hold. Would you check on this and let us know if they will all be released or not?
I will released the first three pallets that were done, this are the ones that were authorized by the customer. I will check what is the issue with the other three, and let you know in a few minutes
Hervin Herrera if you release them, how will we know they need to be mixed? Will you put them on rework status?
I will add the deviation to each pallet and an email will be sent to L3 ops and QA management

Maria Moron Quintero Jorel Gourdin Alan Todd a total of 65960 pieces have been released. The other two LPMs will remain on hold because after the power outage of Friday this two pallets were affected with no lot code on it.
Thanks for the breakdown Maria Moron Quintero! This is very helpful☺️
Alan Todd, Alex Mendoza, Brandon Lance

Team. Here are the current countdowns for each line:
Molding Line 2

We still need to produce 7,850,000 pieces (excluding today's production) to complete the required WIP.Once the WIP is completed, we will switch to FG 30789-Box15, with a planned production of 1,262 cases.
Molding Line 3

Excluding today's production and the WIP currently on HOLD, we still need to produce 2,485,000 pieces to complete the required WIP.Once the WIP is completed, we will switch to FG 30926-Bulk485, requiring 4 pallets at 40 cases per pallet (160 cases total).
Thank you.
